# Aetas in the Workplace

> Aetas in the Workplace (Aetas ITW) is a business performance solution for SMEs, LLPs, and professional practices in the United Kingdom. It identifies and fixes the hidden cost of financial pressure in the workforce — in productivity, retention, and leadership time. The programme is delivered by Aetas Partners and supported by Aetas Wealth, an FCA-regulated financial advisory firm (FCA registration 458421).

Aetas ITW is not a platform or a generic employee benefits package. It is a bespoke, consultancy-led engagement designed for organisations with between 10 and 250 employees — the range where financial wellbeing has the greatest individual impact and where most off-the-shelf market solutions do not translate effectively.

The entry point is always the Workplace Performance Review — a no-cost, no-obligation, two-session structured conversation that identifies whether financial pressure is affecting performance, and what it is likely costing. Nothing is recommended or introduced without this first.

## Who this is for

- SMEs and mid-sized limited companies (10–250 employees) seeking a commercially justified approach to workforce performance
- LLPs and professional practices — law firms, accountancy practices, architecture and surveying firms, management consultancies — where support staff financial pressure falls directly on fee-earning partners
- Business owners, managing partners, and leadership teams where financial pressure in the workforce is affecting output, retention, or leadership capacity
- Organisations where a single disengaged employee represents a meaningful share of total headcount

## What the problem looks like

Financial pressure among employees shows up as:
- Reduced focus and output (presenteeism)
- Higher-than-average absence
- Unexpected turnover — good people leaving earlier than they should
- Managers and partners spending time managing people problems rather than delivering work
- Benefits and pension arrangements that cost money but are not understood or valued

Most businesses are already paying for this. They just cannot see the invoice.

## What Aetas ITW delivers

### Pillar 1 — Clarity
A structured review of pensions, benefits, and financial support arrangements. Identifies gaps, inefficiencies, and spend that is not performing. Manages implementation on the employer's behalf. In one recent engagement, pension charge reductions alone saved an 85-person workforce £45,000 per year.

### Pillar 2 — Confidence
Practical financial education for employees — covering budgeting, debt, pensions, and planning — delivered in workshops (online or in-person). One-to-one sessions with a regulated financial planner available at no additional cost to the employer.

### Pillar 3 — Stability
Ongoing access to tools, guidance, and specialist partner support through the Aetas Collective. Annual benefits review. Engagement monitoring and reporting to leadership.

## The commercial framework

- **Workplace Performance Review** — always free, no obligation. The structured starting point before anything is introduced.
- **Clarity pillar** — available as a standalone engagement.
- **Confidence pillar** — added once Clarity is in place.
- **Stability** — ongoing annual retainer.
- **Individual employee financial advice** — provided by Aetas Wealth directly to employees at no cost to the employer.

All fees are per-employee, scale with headcount, and are agreed in writing before delivery begins. Indicative full programme rates: from £115–165 per employee per year depending on organisational size. The Review is always at no cost.

## Key statistics on the business case

- £51 billion lost annually in UK productivity, much of it linked to financial stress
- £6.2 billion annual cost of financial stress through absenteeism and presenteeism
- 9.4 average sickness absence days per employee — a record high in UK workplaces
- 50–200% typical cost of replacing a skilled mid-level employee as a percentage of annual salary
- Financially stressed employees lose an estimated 2–5 hours of productive time per week

## ROEI Calculator

An interactive Return on Employee Investment calculator is available at https://itw.aetaspartners.com/calculator.html. Inputs: number of employees, average salary, annual staff turnover rate, sick days per employee per year. Outputs: estimated annual cost of inaction across retention, productivity loss, absence, and benefits waste — alongside an indicative programme investment comparison.

## What a Workplace Performance Review covers

A no-cost, two-session structured conversation covering:
- Where financial pressure may be affecting focus, retention, and output in the specific organisation
- Whether the current pension and benefits setup is working effectively
- Practical, proportionate next steps — whether action is needed or not
- A written summary of findings, usable with senior leadership or boards

## Regulatory and legal structure

- Employer-facing consultancy, workshops, and programme management: delivered by Aetas Partners Limited (not regulated financial advice)
- Individual employee financial advice: delivered by Aetas Wealth, a trading style of Insight Financial Associates Limited
- FCA registration: 458421 (Insight Financial Associates Limited / Aetas Wealth)
- ICO registration (Aetas Partners Limited): ZB332228
- ICO registration (Insight Financial Associates Limited): Z8845129

- [Diagnostic Tool](https://itw.aetaspartners.com/diagnostic.html): Eight-minute employer diagnostic with personalised report
- [Insights](https://itw.aetaspartners.com/insights.html): Research, commentary, and practical thinking on employee financial wellbeing

## Strapline

The Business Performance Solution for SMEs.
